It's the home of the Danish royal family and a significant historical site.
It's a famous modern architectural site.
It's inspired by the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales.
It's a world-famous restaurant known for its culinary masterpieces.
It's one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. Make sure to bring a universal adapter if your devices use different plug types.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so it's a good idea to have some local currency on hand.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro, making it a convenient and efficient way to explore the city.
The weather in Copenhagen in December can be quite cold, with temperatures often dropping below -15 degrees Celsius. Make sure to pack warm clothing.

The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen. However, learning a few basic Danish phrases like "hej" (hello), "farvel" (thank you), and "tak" (goodbye) could enhance your experience.
Copenhagen follows left-hand drive. If you plan to rent a car, this is something to keep in mind.
